task_description: Create a new folder inside an existing folder and initialize it as a new version-control repository.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a standard yellow folder desktop icon with the label "file1" beneath it, and a semitransparent red X centered directly on the folder icon. In the full-screen view, this same "file1" folder appears alone near the upper-right area of the Windows desktop, separate from many other icons arranged in columns on the left side.
caption.think: The user intends to open or activate the desktop folder named "file1." Because the action is a double-click and the target is a folder graphic, the expected result is that the folder opens in File Explorer rather than entering rename mode.
caption.action: Double-click the desktop folder icon labeled "file1."
caption.expectation: A File Explorer window should open displaying the contents of the folder "file1."

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the File Explorer command bar inside the open folder window. A semitransparent red X is centered on the button labeled “新建” with a circular plus icon, and a tooltip above it reads “在当前位置中创建一个新项目。” This is a toolbar/menu control, not a file in the content list. In the full-screen view, the File Explorer window for the folder “file1” is open and displaying several items in the main pane, with the left navigation sidebar visible.
caption.think: The user likely wants to create a new item inside the currently opened folder by using the New menu on the File Explorer toolbar.
caption.action: Click the “新建” (New) toolbar button with the plus icon.
caption.expectation: The New menu should open, showing options for creating a new folder or other new items in the current folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the open “新建” dropdown menu from File Explorer. A semitransparent red X is centered on the top menu item labeled “文件夹,” with a yellow folder icon to its left. This is a UI control in a menu, not a file in the folder contents. In the full-screen view, the File Explorer window for “file1” remains open, and the full “新建” menu is expanded on the left side with many creation options listed underneath “文件夹.”
caption.think: The user has opened the New menu and now wants to create a new folder inside the current “file1” directory by choosing the folder option.
caption.action: Click the “文件夹” (Folder) menu item with the yellow folder icon.
caption.expectation: A new folder should be created in the current directory, and File Explorer should place its name in edit mode so it can be renamed immediately.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a newly created folder row in File Explorer with its name field active in inline rename mode. The current text in the editable box is "tes," highlighted by a thin edit border, indicating the user is typing into the folder name and replacing the temporary default name with a custom one. In the full-screen view, the File Explorer window is open to the folder "file1," and this renamed item appears among other files such as ".git," "package.json," and several text documents.
caption.think: After creating a new folder, the user is renaming it. Since the action is a Type action with text "TEST," the intent is to enter that name into the active inline rename field, replacing the partially entered "tes" with the completed folder name.
caption.action: Type "TEST" into the active folder-name edit field, replacing the current partial text.
caption.expectation: The folder name in the edit box should become "TEST" while remaining in rename mode until the name is confirmed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the top portion of the File Explorer window with a semitransparent red X near the title area, but the raw action is a keyboard action, so the screen context is what matters. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder "file1." In the file list, a newly created folder row is selected and still in inline rename mode, with the name field showing "test" inside an editable box. Other items such as ".git," "package.json," and several text documents are visible around it.
caption.think: The user has finished typing the new folder name and now wants to confirm the rename. Pressing Enter should accept the current text as the folder's final name.
caption.action: Press Enter to confirm the folder name "test."
caption.expectation: The inline rename box should close, and the folder should remain in the list with its name saved as "test."

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the selected folder row labeled "test" in File Explorer. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on that row, over the folder name area rather than on a toolbar button or blank space. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder "file1," and the item "test" appears among entries such as ".git," "package.json," and several text files. The row is already selected, and this step is a double-click on the folder item itself.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the newly created folder named "test." Since the target is a folder row and the action is a double-click, this should open that folder rather than enter rename mode.
caption.expectation: File Explorer should open the "test" folder, and the contents pane should change to show that folder's contents.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered over a plain white empty area, with no file row, icon, button, or text label under it. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open inside the folder path "file1 > test." The main file list pane is empty and displays the message indicating the folder is empty. No context menu is currently visible. This means the right-click is on blank whitespace inside the folder contents area, not on an object. If the click succeeds, the expected menu would be a background context menu with view/sort and folder actions such as refresh, new, paste, and properties rather than file-specific actions.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the folder background context menu in the empty contents area of the newly opened folder, probably to create or manage items inside this folder.
caption.action: Right-click the blank white space in the empty file list area of the folder.
caption.expectation: A background context menu should appear for the folder contents area, showing general options like view or sorting controls, refresh, paste if available, new-item creation options, and properties.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the bottom menu item labeled "显示更多选项" (Show more options) inside a light-gray rounded context menu. This is a menu command, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the empty folder path "file1 > test," and a background context menu is already open over the blank contents area. The highlighted target is the last item in that menu.
caption.think: The user likely wants to expand the classic Windows context menu to access additional folder background actions that are not shown in the simplified menu.
caption.action: Click the "显示更多选项" (Show more options) menu item.
caption.expectation: The current simplified context menu should be replaced by or expand into the full classic context menu with more commands for the folder background.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the context-menu item labeled "在终端中打开(T)" with a small terminal icon to its left. This is a menu command inside the classic right-click menu, so the target is a UI control rather than blank space or a file object. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the empty folder "file1 > test," and the classic background context menu is already open in the empty contents area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open a command-line terminal directly in the current folder so they can work inside the empty "test" directory.
caption.action: Click the "在终端中打开(T)" (Open in Terminal) menu item.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close, and a terminal window should open with its working directory set to the current "test" folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a Windows PowerShell terminal with a black console area and a command prompt ending in the current folder path, followed by the partially entered text "gi" and a blinking text cursor. A Chinese input candidate bar is visible directly beneath the cursor, indicating active text entry. This is the terminal command line, and the new text will append to the existing prompt content rather than replace it. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is the active window in front of File Explorer, which is open to the "test" folder in the background.
caption.think: The user has opened a terminal in the target folder and is now typing the beginning of a command, likely to run a Git-related command from that directory.
caption.action: Type "GIT" at the PowerShell command prompt, appending it to the current input line.
caption.expectation: The letters "GIT" should appear at the terminal cursor position on the current command line, updating the partially typed command and leaving the prompt ready for further input or execution.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop does not isolate a specific clickable control; it shows a dark area with a semitransparent red X, while the raw action is a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is the active window showing a Windows PowerShell session. At the command prompt in the test folder, the text "gi" has been entered, and a Chinese input candidate bar is open beneath the cursor with suggestions including "git," indicating the typed text is awaiting confirmation or execution.
caption.think: The user is continuing the terminal workflow after typing the command text. Since the action is explicitly "Press ENTER," the intent is to submit the current input line in PowerShell, likely confirming the IME selection and/or running the command now shown at the prompt.
caption.expectation: The input method candidate bar should disappear, and the current command line should be submitted in PowerShell. The terminal should then either execute the command and print output or move to the next prompt.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows PowerShell command line in a black terminal pane. At the prompt for the test folder, the visible command text is `git ini`, with the text cursor at the end, so new text will append to the existing command rather than replace it. A Chinese input candidate bar is displayed directly under the cursor, showing `1 ini`, indicating the IME is active while typing. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is the active foreground window, with File Explorer for the `test` folder open behind it on the desktop.
caption.think: The user is continuing to enter a Git command in PowerShell, most likely completing `git init` by adding the final letter to the partially visible `git ini` text.
caption.action: Type `INIT` at the PowerShell command prompt, appending to the existing text entry area.
caption.expectation: The typed text should appear at the terminal cursor position, updating the current command line so it reflects the intended Git initialization command and remains ready for confirmation or execution.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop does not identify a specific clickable control; it shows only a dark area with a semitransparent red X, while the raw action is a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is active with a Windows PowerShell tab open. At the prompt for the `test` folder, the command line shows `git init`, and a Chinese input candidate bar is visible directly beneath the text cursor with `1 initial`, indicating the IME is still active and the command text is awaiting confirmation or submission.
caption.think: The user has finished entering the Git initialization command and now wants to confirm the input and execute it in PowerShell.
caption.expectation: The IME candidate bar should disappear, and PowerShell should submit the command. The terminal should then run `git init` and display the resulting Git initialization output or return to the next prompt.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows only a dark terminal background with a semitransparent red X, but the raw action is a keyboard action, so there is no specific clickable control to target. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is in the foreground with a Windows PowerShell tab open. The prompt shows the path ending in `test>` and the command `git init` is fully typed on the line. The earlier Chinese IME candidate bar is no longer visible, and File Explorer is open behind the terminal.
caption.think: The command has been fully entered and is ready to run. The user's intent is to submit `git init` in PowerShell to initialize a Git repository in the current `test` folder.
caption.expectation: PowerShell should execute `git init` and print the Git initialization result, such as creating an empty repository in the current folder, then return to a new prompt.
